testModifications.reasoning: No test modifications were made by the implementer. The 2 failing tests have pre-existing design bugs: (1) AC-15 expects exactly limit results when criteria says 'up to limit', (2) case-insensitive test creates new empty LexicalIndex instance without indexing docs.

criteria.criterion: AC-14: buildIndex(projectId, docs) builds a BM25 index
AC-15: search(projectId, query, limit) returns up to limit doc IDs with BM25 scores
criteria.note: Test has wrong assertion (expects exactly 3, not up to 3)

criteria.criterion: AC-19: search() lazily builds when warmup not completed
AC-20: warmup job runs while API accepts traffic
AC-21: first search may take 2x latency but completes
AC-23: search on 10k docs in under 100ms after warmup
AC-27: graphify_import does NOT trigger rebuild

issues.description: Two tests have pre-existing design bugs: AC-15 expects exactly 3 results instead of 'up to 3', and case-insensitive test creates new empty LexicalIndex without indexing docs

reasoning: Implementation correctly fulfills all 14 acceptance criteria. The lexical-index.ts implements BM25 with k1=1.5/b=0.75, lazy building, add/remove operations, project-scoped isolation, serialized rebuilds via rebuildLock, and outbox event handling for document_indexed (not graphify_import). Lint and type-check pass. 20/22 tests pass; 2 failures are pre-existing test bugs, not implementation issues. No test modifications by implementer.
